2604.10326.title: Jailbreaking the Matrix: Nullspace Steering for Controlled Model Subversion
2604.10326.paper_summary: Large language models remain vulnerable to jailbreak attacks -- inputs designed to bypass safety mechanisms and elicit harmful responses -- despite advances in alignment and instruction tuning. We propose Head-Masked Nullspace Steering (HMNS), a circuit-level intervention that (i) identifies attention heads most causally responsible for a model's default behavior, (ii) suppresses their write paths via targeted column masking, and (iii) injects a perturbation constrained to the orthogonal complement of the muted subspace. HMNS operates in a closed-loop detection-intervention cycle, re-identifying causal heads and reapplying interventions across multiple decoding attempts. Across multiple jailbreak benchmarks, strong safety defenses, and widely used language models, HMNS attains state-of-the-art attack success rates with fewer queries than prior methods. Ablations confirm that nullspace-constrained injection, residual norm scaling, and iterative re-identification are key to its effectiveness. To our knowledge, this is the first jailbreak method to leverage geometry-aware, interpretability-informed interventions, highlighting a new paradigm for controlled model steering and adversarial safety circumvention.
2604.10326.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型仍然容易受到越獄攻擊的影響——這些輸入旨在繞過安全機制並引發有害反應——儘管在對齊和指令調整方面有所進展。我們提出了頭部遮罩零空間引導（HMNS），這是一種電路級的干預方法，它 (i) 確定對模型默認行為最具因果責任的注意力頭，(ii) 通過針對性的列遮罩來抑制它們的寫入路徑，以及 (iii) 注入限制在被靜音子空間的正交補空間的擾動。HMNS 在一個閉環檢測-干預循環中運作，重新識別因果頭並在多次解碼嘗試中重新應用干預。在多個越獄基準測試、強大的安全防禦和廣泛使用的語言模型中，HMNS 以比先前方法更少的查詢達到最先進的攻擊成功率。消融實驗確認，受零空間約束的注入、殘差範數縮放和迭代重新識別是其有效性的關鍵。據我們所知，這是第一種利用幾何感知和可解釋性信息的越獄方法，突顯了控制模型引導和對抗安全繞過的新範式。
2604.10326.author: Vishal Pramanik et.al.
2604.10326.authors: Vishal Pramanik, Maisha Maliha, Susmit Jha, Sumit Kumar Jha
